title: malva pallida
intro: Malva pallida, also known as the marsh mallow or common mallow, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive pink or white flowers and soft, velvety leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which are attributed to its high mucilage content. Consuming Malva pallida is said to provide numerous health benefits, including reducing digestive issues, alleviating respiratory problems, and soothing skin irritations. Malva pallida is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, often in combination with other herbs, to promote relaxation and support overall well-being.
